memories.memory_text: I remember the plane jolting hard, nearly knocking my karate book out of my hand, and how I automatically tossed my hair out of my eyes—same thing I always do when I'm scared.
grounded_facts: The plane experienced sudden turbulence, causing a violent bump.
Dale was reading a book titled 'KARATE FOR THE SINGLE GIRL.... A Guide to Survival In The City.'
She reacted to the turbulence with a habitual gesture of tossing her hair out of her eyes.
She looked out the window with sudden fright but saw only clouds.
She then looked forward and observed Flash Gordon standing calmly in the cockpit doorway.

memories.memory_text: I snapped at Flash to shut up when the plane was shaking—there was no time for his suggestions, not when the pilots were already struggling to keep us in the air.
grounded_facts: Dale Arden told Flash to 'SHUT UP' during turbulence in the plane.
The plane experienced multiple violent jolts while in flight.
Dale observed that the pilots had their hands full and needed to focus.

memories.memory_text: I snapped at Flash when he offered me a candy bar—like I’d take sweets from a stranger—but then I couldn’t help it, I asked if I could still have it after admitting how much I hate flying.
grounded_facts: Dale initially refuses Flash's offer of a candy bar, expressing skepticism.
She identifies herself as a travel agent and voices her fear of flying.
Moments later, she reverses her refusal and asks to have the candy bar.

memories.memory_text: I gasped when I realized the plane might be moving backward—then I saw the clouds rushing past and shouted, 'Holy cow! Look at the clouds!'—it felt like we were falling.
grounded_facts: Dale expresses alarm, questioning if the plane is going backward.
She reacts with visible shock and awe upon seeing the clouds move rapidly outside the window.

memories.memory_text: I remember the light—cold and strange—washing over my face, and I couldn't speak, couldn't move, just clung to Flash's hand like it was the only real thing left.
grounded_facts: Dale's face was bathed in an eerie light.
Dale grasped Flash's hand tightly during the event.
Dale was speechless during the phenomenon.

memories.memory_text: I remember stumbling into the cockpit, the wind tearing at me like a living thing, and seeing Flash fighting the controls as the plane plunged downward.
grounded_facts: Dale enters the cockpit during a violent windstorm caused by shattered windows. She physically struggles against the wind while moving toward the co-pilot's seat. The plane is in a steep dive
and Flash is already at the controls trying to regain control. Flash shouts for her to grab the other wheel to help pull up. Dale responds by taking the co-pilot's controls and pulling back.

memories.memory_text: As we strained on the yoke together, I cracked that joke about the man falling ninety stories—Flash didn’t know it, and for a second, the terror felt lighter.
grounded_facts: Dale pulls back on the co-pilot's control wheel in sync with Flash. She initiates a darkly humorous anecdote to cope with the extreme stress. Flash responds with interest
asking how the man survived. Dale delivers the punchline about the window being ninety-one stories up. The moment occurs mid-crisis
while the plane is still diving and lightning strikes nearby.

memories.memory_text: I recognized him from a PEOPLE magazine article after he left NASA — something about him being unstable, and the way he was smiling just confirmed it.
grounded_facts: Dale saw Dr. Hans Zarkov in a PEOPLE magazine after he left NASA
The article implied Zarkov was mentally unstable
Dale associates Zarkov's strange smile with danger

memories.memory_text: He pressed the gun into my back and I felt the heat from those red laser bolts — I couldn't move, I just ran when he shoved me toward the stairs.
grounded_facts: Zarkov pointed a gun at Flash and then jabbed it into Dale's back
Red laser bolts struck the greenhouse, causing explosions and fire
Dale experienced physical pressure from the gun and sensory input from the chaos

memories.memory_text: I gasped and shrank back when Zarkov ripped open that shiny capsule and pointed his gun at us, yelling about dying in space — then he shoved me inside like I didn’t even matter.
grounded_facts: Dale gasps and shrinks back in fear when Zarkov reveals the capsule and threatens them.
Zarkov physically pushes Dale into the capsule against her will.
Dale verbally resists, stating she doesn't book kamikaze tours.

memories.memory_text: I shouted at Flash to take Zarkov down—he was forcing us into this thing like a madman, and I couldn’t just sit there.
grounded_facts: Dale yelled 'Flash! Sack him!' while Zarkov was setting switches and pointing a gun.
Zarkov was armed and directing Flash to sit and place his foot on a red pedal.
Dale initiated the call to action before Flash attacked.

memories.memory_text: The spinning got so strong I was crushed against Flash, my back pressed to his chest—I couldn’t move, barely breathe, like the force was molding us together.
grounded_facts: The capsule began to whirl after Zarkov hit a switch, creating increasing centrifugal force.
Dale and Flash were thrown against the wall, with Dale pressed tightly against Flash.
The force intensified to the point of physical immobilization and visible strain.

memories.memory_text: I remember clinging to Flash as we tumbled through that hellish fire—my head spinning, the world just a blur of amber and flame, and then that sudden, impossible rise above the lava.
grounded_facts: Dale was inside the capsule during atmospheric descent.
She was held by Flash and appeared semi-conscious.
She experienced the visual of Mongo's molten surface through the transparent walls.
The capsule narrowly avoided destruction by changing course above the flames.
She was present as the capsule entered a new atmosphere with colored clouds and descended into grass.

memories.memory_text: I remember the cold snap of the manacle locking around my wrist, the red armored men moving fast—no warning, just hands grabbing me, pulling me down as if I were nothing.
grounded_facts: Dale was forcibly handcuffed by the armored men immediately after exiting the capsule.
The action happened suddenly, without warning, during a silent standoff.
She was restrained alongside Zarkov while Flash was also captured.

memories.memory_text: I caught a glimpse of that poor man's torn body and the beast being tormented—then I shut my eyes tight and tried to pretend we were on a stupid vacation to Disneyland instead.
grounded_facts: Dale sees the torn body of a man and a tethered beast being tormented by Mongo men through an opening.
She turns her head away and shuts her eyes while being marched along.
She tells Flash she is shutting her eyes and dreaming they are going to Disneyland.

memories.memory_text: I fought hard, kicking and striking with everything I had, but there were too many of them — I remember the cold grip of their hands dragging me up the stairs, screaming Flash’s name into the dark.
grounded_facts: Dale engaged in a physical fight using karate.
She was overpowered by the Amazon Dykes.
She was physically dragged up a flight of stairs.
She shouted 'Flash!!' during the capture.

memories.memory_text: I was reaching down, straining to hold Flash's hand—our fingertips just barely touched, and for a second, I thought I could pull him up.
grounded_facts: Dale was physically struggling to reach Flash while being restrained by Amazon guards.
Their fingertips made contact before Flash disappeared.

memories.memory_text: I saw the step beneath Flash open like a mouth and swallow him whole—he screamed and then vanished, and that woman Aura jumped in after him before the door snapped shut.
grounded_facts: Flash fell through a trapdoor in the staircase.
Aura ran into the shot and jumped into the same trapdoor just before it closed.

memories.memory_text: I remember the fear creeping in when I realized Ming might force me into marriage—becoming his consort wasn't rescue, it was another kind of prison.
grounded_facts: Dale Arden is not physically present in the scene, but the revelation that Emperor Ming intends to marry her is discussed by Aura and Flash.
The marriage is framed as a strategic move by Ming, indicating a shift in Dale's status from captive to a symbolic figure of power.
Dale's potential marriage to Ming is presented as a threat, aligning with her trait of fearfulness and her constraint to react with fear to danger.

memories.memory_text: I knocked the flagon out of her hand with a karate chop when she tried to make me drink that green stuff—like hell I’d just obey them.
grounded_facts: Dale physically resists Hedonia by knocking the flagon away with a karate chop.
Dale expresses fear and defiance by questioning the liquid and rejecting it.

memories.memory_text: I screamed for Flash, but no one answered—and then I just broke, grabbed the flagon and drank it, surprised at how good it tasted.
grounded_facts: Dale calls out for Flash with no response.
Dale experiences visible despair, then voluntarily drinks the green liquid.
Dale comments that the liquid 'isn't bad at all' after drinking it.

memories.memory_text: Flash asked Aura to let him use the thought-transfer device to contact me, and said 'Please' when she teased him about not showing him how.
grounded_facts: Flash asked Aura if he could use the telekinetic thought transfer device to contact Dale.
Aura responded to Flash's request by saying 'If I showed you how' and then 'But I'm not going to'.
Flash said 'Please' in response to Aura's refusal.

memories.scene_title: 56、INT. CUTTING BETWEEN BATHING AREA AND COCKPIT
memories.memory_text: I was sitting naked in that bathtub, feeling completely exposed, when Flash’s voice suddenly came into my head—telling me to fake compliance and free Dr. Zarkov before Ming could get to me.
grounded_facts: Dale is in a bathtub
unclothed
while being prepared for Ming. Flash communicates telepathically
instructing her to pretend cooperation and rescue Zarkov. Dale confirms she will attempt the plan despite its difficulty.

memories.scene_title: 56、INT. CUTTING BETWEEN BATHING AREA AND COCKPIT
memories.memory_text: When Flash asked if I could get what he was thinking, I said yes. I understood him without words.
grounded_facts: Flash asks Dale if she understands his thoughts.
Dale responds affirmatively in a heightened tone.
The exchange implies a close, intuitive connection between Dale and Flash.

memories.memory_text: I smiled and told her I was ready, then waited until she leaned over the bed—when her guard was down, I struck fast with a karate-chop to make sure she wouldn’t interfere.
grounded_facts: Dale is in Ming's bedchamber, reclining on a large bed in dim light.
A serving girl approaches with a flagon of liquid and informs Dale that someone is coming to see her.
Dale responds calmly, asking the girl to turn down the light.
As the serving girl leans over the bed to adjust the light, Dale delivers a swift karate-chop to incapacitate her.

memories.memory_text: I slid the tray down and lifted my skirt just enough—he couldn’t look away. I saw the hunger in his eyes before he even moved.
grounded_facts: Dale posed as a serving girl to approach the guard.
She put down the tray and lifted her skirt above her knee to distract the guard.
The guard reacted with visible interest and stepped toward her.

memories.memory_text: One kick to his gut, then three sharp chops to the neck—he crumpled fast. I was already yanking the keys off his belt before he hit the floor.
grounded_facts: Dale kicked the guard in the groin, causing him to double over.
She followed with a series of karate chops to his neck, incapacitating him.
She immediately took the key ring from his belt after he fell.

memories.memory_text: I remember demanding that Zarkov make the capsule fly—we had to save Flash from that vampire—then suddenly, a blinding blue light erupted all around us.
grounded_facts: Dale asks Zarkov if the capsule will fly.
Dale insists it has to fly because they need to rescue Flash from a vampire.
A sudden blinding blue light fills the capsule, interrupting the moment.

memories.scene_title: 90、EXT. SKY PALACE - VULTAN'S EYRIE - A HIGH TERRACE
memories.memory_text: I slapped his hand away when he touched me—his fingers were rough, and I could smell the metal of his armor as he leaned in, leering. I told him to watch out, that Ming wanted to marry me, but my voice shook even as I said it.
grounded_facts: Vultan touched Dale's bosom.
Dale slapped Vultan's hand away.
Dale warned Vultan that Ming wanted to marry her.
Vultan reacted with amusement ('hoots') to Dale's warning.
Hawkgirls were present and watching.

memories.memory_text: I shouted at them—those war-obsessed Mongo leaders—asking if they were all crazy, demanding they try teamwork for once instead of just fighting each other.
grounded_facts: Dale speaks out in frustration during a tense confrontation on Vultan's terrace.
She directly questions the Mongo leaders' lack of cooperation, referencing teamwork in response to their hostility.
Her line 'Is everyone on Mongo CRAZY? Haven't you guys ever heard of TEAMWORK?' is spoken in a moment of emotional exasperation.

memories.memory_text: I saw the soldiers grab my Hawkman, smash his wings, and throw him out—I couldn't do anything, just backed away and dropped into a stance, heart pounding.
grounded_facts: Dale witnessed soldiers seizing the Hawkman and violently ejecting him from the ship
Dale reacted by backing off and assuming a karate stance
The Hawkman was attacked and thrown out through the bottom of the ship while Dale was present

memories.memory_text: Hedonia walked in smiling, holding yards of white fabric, and said, 'Your wedding dress'—I froze, not knowing whether to scream or run.
grounded_facts: Hedonia entered with arms full of white material described as a wedding dress
Hedonia stated
Your wedding dress
in the context of fitting Dale
Dale was present and reacted with shock to the announcement

memories.scene_title: 136、INT. MING'S GREAT HALL - HIGH WIDE ANGLE
memories.memory_text: I walked down the hall on Klytus's arm, the Amazons prodding me forward, their spears at my back, while girls carried my train and Ming stared at me like I was already his.
grounded_facts: Dale is escorted by Klytus with Amazons marching behind her.
Amazons carry spears and are positioned at Dale's back.
Lovely girls bear the train of Dale's wedding gown.
Ming is present at the throne-altar, watching Dale with a lecherous gaze.
Dale is visibly crying, with tears rolling silently down her cheeks.

memories.memory_text: I was going over a play in my head when the plane jolted hard—next thing I knew, I was on my feet, leaning into the cockpit to check on the pilots.
grounded_facts: Flash was sitting in the cabin studying a football play-sheet when the plane experienced sudden turbulence.
The plane made a violent bump, startling the other passenger, Dale Arden.
Flash immediately stood up and moved to the cockpit doorway to speak with the pilots.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed the rack above Dale when the plane jolted, then sat beside her and offered my candy bar—she shot me a look like I was some creep, but I told her I wasn't a stranger, not with my face on People magazine.
grounded_facts: Flash was thrown off balance by turbulence and caught the overhead rack.
He sat next to Dale and offered her a candy bar to help with her nerves.
He referenced knowing his name as a reason he wasn't a stranger.
Dale initially refused the candy bar, citing not taking food from strangers.

memories.memory_text: She asked if we were going backward, then gasped and pointed at the clouds—her voice was tight with fear, and I could feel the plane lurching, something wasn't right.
grounded_facts: Dale asked Flash if the plane was going backward.
Dale exclaimed 'Holy cow! Look at the clouds!' with visible alarm.
The plane was experiencing severe buffeting during the exchange.
Flash remained seated next to Dale during the event.

memories.memory_text: The sky turned red and the clouds started moving impossibly fast around the plane — I could feel Dale squeezing my hand like she was afraid she'd vanish.
grounded_facts: The sky turned red and fast-moving clouds surrounded the plane.
Dale grasped Flash's hand with all her strength.
Flash and Dale's faces were bathed in eerie red light.

memories.memory_text: I remember the cockpit door exploding open, wind tearing through the cabin, papers flying everywhere—I was on my feet in an instant, unbuckling and moving forward fast.
grounded_facts: The cockpit door was violently blown off its hinges.
A strong wind entered the cabin, scattering Flash's play sheets.
Flash immediately unsnapped his belt, stood up, and rushed toward the cockpit.

memories.memory_text: I lunged into the cockpit and grabbed the bucking yoke, fighting to pull the plane out of the dive as wind tore through the shattered windows.
grounded_facts: Flash entered the cockpit after the pilots vanished
He took the left-hand seat and physically struggled with the controls
He was actively resisting the plane's descent by pulling back on the yoke and using the rudder pedals

memories.memory_text: Dale shouted some dumb joke about a man falling ninety stories while we were both yanking the controls, and for a second it cut through the panic—I actually wanted to hear how it ended.
grounded_facts: Dale told a joke during the crisis
Flash responded verbally to the joke while still fighting the controls
The exchange occurred while both were attempting to pull the plane up

memories.memory_text: I shouted to brace ourselves—I was locking onto that field up ahead as the only shot we had to land this thing.
grounded_facts: Flash is in the cockpit of a plane during a critical moment.
Flash identifies a field as a potential landing site.
Flash gives a warning for others to brace, indicating immediate action.
Flash is taking control of the situation by aiming the plane.

memories.memory_text: I remember Dale suddenly stopping, staring at Zarkov like she’d seen a ghost, and then trying to back out fast—something about a magazine, NASA, and almost saying he was insane. I didn’t get it at first, but her look told me everything.
grounded_facts: Dale recognizes Dr. Zarkov from a PEOPLE magazine article.
Dale hesitates while speaking, nearly saying Zarkov is insane.
Dale urgently suggests leaving to find another phone.
Flash notices Dale's alarmed expression and urgency despite not understanding immediately.

memories.memory_text: One second we were talking, the next—Zarkov pulls a gun and shoves it into Dale’s back, yelling about Earth being attacked. Red laser bolts are exploding the greenhouse around us, glass raining down, fire everywhere, and he’s jabbing me with the revolver, screaming 'Up the stairs—RUN!'
grounded_facts: Zarkov points a gun at Flash and forces them up the stairs.
Zarkov physically prods Dale with the revolver.
Red laser bolts attack the greenhouse, shattering glass and igniting flames.
Flash whispers to Dale to go with the flow and wait for a chance while running.
The staircase begins disintegrating from a red energy blast below.

memories.memory_text: I saw Zarkov shove Dale into that mirror-bright capsule and I didn't think—I just leapt after her, the world exploding around us.
grounded_facts: Zarkov pushed Dale into the capsule.
Flash reacted immediately by leaping into the capsule after Dale.
The area was under heavy attack with explosions and energy bolts striking nearby.
One energy bolt hit the capsule directly but left no mark.

memories.memory_text: I jumped Zarkov, punched him in the gut, and tried to stop him from launching, but he slammed me back and hit a switch that made the capsule start whirling.
grounded_facts: Flash physically attacked Zarkov by punching him in the gut.
Zarkov reacted with force and avoided being subdued.
The capsule began to whirl after Zarkov hit a switch while evading Flash.

memories.memory_text: The spinning got worse, I grabbed Dale and held her close as the force slammed us against the wall—our bodies pressed together, barely able to move.
grounded_facts: Flash located Dale and wrapped his arms around her protectively.
Increasing centrifugal force threw both Flash and Dale against the capsule wall.
Their bodies were pressed tightly together due to the spinning motion.

memories.memory_text: I remember holding Dale as we crashed through the fire, the capsule spinning, then slowing—my arms were locked around her, and when I saw Mongo’s molten surface rushing up, I thought we were dead.
grounded_facts: Flash is holding Dale during the capsule's descent.
The capsule experiences fiery descent through Mongo's atmosphere with visible lava and flames.
Flash is semi-conscious, crumpled against the wall, but physically protective of Dale.
The capsule changes course just before impact and emerges above a new landscape.

memories.memory_text: I stepped forward, smiled, and said 'Hi!'—reaching out my hand like it was a normal first meeting, but the next thing I knew, a manacle snapped onto my wrist and I was slammed to the ground.
grounded_facts: Flash initiated contact by greeting the alien leader and extending his hand.
Immediately after saying 'Hi!', Flash was restrained with a manacle and thrown to the ground.
Flash expressed sarcasm toward Zarkov after being captured, indicating he remembered Zarkov's suggestion to extend his hand.

memories.scene_title: 33、INT. PALACE OF HALLS - SERIES OF DISSOLVES
memories.memory_text: I grabbed Zarkov and shouted in his face—'You got us into this—HOW DO WE GET OUT?'—because we were being dragged through some twisted hall by armored goons and he was still yammering about Black Holes like it was a damn science fair.
grounded_facts: Flash is being marched by armored men through a disorienting, curvilinear space.
Zarkov makes a comment about traveling through a Black Hole and arriving on Mongo.
Flash responds directly to Zarkov with frustration, blaming him for their capture.

memories.scene_title: 33、INT. PALACE OF HALLS - SERIES OF DISSOLVES
memories.memory_text: I don’t know what Ming said next, but the way those armored men tightened their grip and the air went cold—I knew they were about to execute us. I locked eyes with him, ready to fight even if it was the last thing I did.
grounded_facts: Flash is in the presence of Ming and is being marched under guard.
The environment is oppressive and disorienting, contributing to a sense of impending doom.
Flash perceives a shift in tension indicating an execution order may have been given.

memories.memory_text: I was on the floor, completely overwhelmed—dozens of Amazons on top of me, pressing me down, and I could see Dale being dragged up the stairs, helpless.
grounded_facts: Flash is overpowered and pinned to the floor by multiple Amazons
He visually sees Dale being dragged up a flight of stairs
He observes her with despairing eyes

memories.memory_text: I heard her scream my name—'Flash!!'—and something inside me snapped; I just exploded upward and broke free, charging after her.
grounded_facts: Flash hears Dale shout 'Flash!!' in distress
This triggers a surge of adrenaline
He exerts a superhuman effort to break free from the Amazons
He immediately rushes after Dale

memories.memory_text: I was so close to Dale—our fingertips just brushed—and then the step beneath me vanished, and I was falling, screaming into the dark.
grounded_facts: Flash struggles to reach Dale on the staircase.
Their fingertips make momentary contact.
The step beneath Flash opens as a trap door.
Flash falls through with a yell.

memories.scene_title: 37、INT. A DARK METALLIC SHAFT - SHOOTING DOWN IT
memories.memory_text: I was falling through this dark, endless shaft, and all I could hear was Aura yelling to take a breath and grab her ankle when we hit the water—then suddenly, a massive splash swallowed everything.
grounded_facts: Flash is falling in a dark metallic shaft with Aura.
Aura instructs Flash to take a deep breath and grab her ankle after hitting the water.
Flash questions what water she means, and immediately a loud, massive splash is heard.

memories.memory_text: I saw it through the glass—the lake of seething magma, glowing like hell itself, spinning slow and hot, feeding that weapon aimed at Earth. Aura said it was the plasma core, unstoppable, but I knew we had to try.
grounded_facts: Flash sees a lake of seething magma through tinted glass in an underground corridor.
He learns from Aura that the plasma core powers Ming's energy beam aimed at Earth.
Flash reacts with shock and determination upon hearing this.

memories.memory_text: The Lizard Men—chained, shoveling gravel into the magma, guards shocking them when they fall. If they couldn't get up, those hooded men just threw them in. I saw one collapse… and vanish into the fire.
grounded_facts: Flash witnesses Lizard Men enslaved, shackled with ankle electrodes, and forced to shovel radioactive gravel.
He sees guards on a catwalk using shocks to revive collapsed slaves.
He observes hooded men throwing unresponsive slaves into the magma.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Aura’s shoulders and demanded to know where Dale was—then she pulled me into a niche as soldiers passed, her body pressed against mine, and whispered that Ming intends to marry Dale. I could barely process it—Ming marrying Dale? It hit me like a shock I couldn’t shake.
grounded_facts: Flash stopped Aura to ask about Dale's location.
Aura pulled Flash into a niche to hide from soldiers.
Aura whispered that Ming intends to marry Dale.
Flash reacted with disbelief and shock to the news.

memories.memory_text: I looked through the glass doors and saw rocket ships inside—real ones—and when Aura said I’d fly them, I snapped, 'I can’t fly a damn rocket ship!' Her teasing reply barely registered; all I could think was this mission just got a thousand times riskier.
grounded_facts: Flash saw rocket ships through glass doors.
Aura told Flash he would fly the ship.
Flash explicitly stated he could not fly a rocket ship.
Aura responded with a soft, teasing tone, offering to teach him.

memories.memory_text: I asked Aura why the moon kingdoms don’t team up against Ming, and then it hit me—what if they could? That moment, I saw it: not just fighting, but uniting as a real strategy.
grounded_facts: Flash questions why the moon kingdoms don't team up against Ming.
Flash has a sudden thoughtful look, indicating a realization about teamwork.

memories.memory_text: I slammed my hand on the controls, sent the ship into a dive—Aura screamed, dropped the telepathic hood. I grabbed her wrists before she could stop me. I had to force her to let me reach Dale.
grounded_facts: Flash deliberately causes the craft to enter a violent dive by manipulating the control panel.
Aura drops the telepathic hood and attempts to regain control.
Flash seizes Aura's wrists to prevent her from reaching the controls.

memories.memory_text: I had to hold Aura's wrists tight—she was fighting me, but I couldn't let go until she connected me to Dale. The planet was rushing up at us, but I didn't care—I wasn't losing her again.
grounded_facts: Flash physically restrained Aura by gripping her wrists.
Flash demanded Aura use a device to connect him to Dale Arden.
The ship was descending rapidly toward Ardentia during the confrontation.

memories.scene_title: 56、INT. CUTTING BETWEEN BATHING AREA AND COCKPIT
memories.memory_text: I told Dale to fake them out and get to Zarkov—she was scared, chained up and about to be handed over to Ming, but she had to move fast and stay sharp.
grounded_facts: Flash instructed Dale to deceive Ming's forces and rescue Dr. Zarkov.
Flash knew Dale was in danger and being prepared for Ming.
Flash believed help would arrive soon and coordinated a plan with Dale.

memories.scene_title: 56、INT. CUTTING BETWEEN BATHING AREA AND COCKPIT
memories.memory_text: When she said she was naked in a bathtub, I couldn't breathe—my voice broke, and for a second all I wanted was to touch her; then I just held on to the thought that she understood me, even without words.
grounded_facts: Flash reacted emotionally upon hearing Dale was naked in a bathtub.
Flash expressed longing to see and touch Dale.
Flash asked if Dale understood his unspoken thoughts, and she affirmed it.

memories.memory_text: I stared into Aura's eyes and almost sent Dale a final message, but then I realized it was pointless—sending words through her would be like handing a lettuce-leaf to a rabbit.
grounded_facts: Flash considers sending a message to Dale through Aura.
He reconsiders, expressing distrust in Aura delivering the message.

memories.memory_text: I jammed my hand into one of the serpent stump's holes, left it just long enough to make everyone think the worst, then yanked it out and threw the challenge back at Barin—'Your turn, Prince Barin.'
grounded_facts: Flash chooses to face the serpent stump trial.
He dramatically removes his hand unharmed and dares Barin to go next.

memories.memory_text: I called out to Dale mid-flight, telling her we were gonna do it first thing when we landed—she smiled and said I’d make her blush, like we had time for shyness with everything falling apart around us.
grounded_facts: Flash speaks to Dale while being carried by a Hawkman, separated from her by about a hundred feet.
He initiates a romantic exchange by saying 'Guess the first thing you and me are gonna do after we land.'
Dale responds playfully, calling him 'Darling' and teasing him about making her blush.

memories.memory_text: One second she was there, then a shadow fell over her—next thing I saw was that glow pulling her and her Hawkman up into a ship; I screamed 'I'm coming, Dale!' and told my Hawkman to climb, but he dove instead.
grounded_facts: A dark shadow falls over Dale, followed by a flickering ray and glowing light enveloping her and her Hawkman.
Dale is visibly and suddenly pulled upward toward a hovering rocket ship.
Flash shouts 'I'm coming, Dale!' and commands his Hawkman to follow and climb, but the Hawkman instead dives vertically in obedience to Vultan.

memories.memory_text: I climbed out of the capsule and heard Barin shout that I was casting a shadow—completely invisible, but my shadow was still there on the ground.
grounded_facts: Flash exits the capsule after being exposed to blinding blue light.
Flash is completely invisible but still casts a visible shadow on the ground.
Barin observes and verbally reacts to Flash's shadow.

memories.memory_text: I snatched the ring from Ming’s hand and slammed it onto Dale’s finger—her gasp cut through the silence, and for a heartbeat, I knew she felt me there.
grounded_facts: An invisible hand takes the ring from Ming and places it on Dale's finger.
Dale reacts with surprise, shouting 'Flash!!!' after the ring is placed.
The act interrupts the wedding ritual and triggers chaos.

memories.memory_text: I drew the sword from the crystal block and held it before me. When it appeared like fire, Ming reacted sharply and said only his heir could pull it.
grounded_facts: Flash reveals a sword identical to Ming's, described as a blade of fire appearing to hang in the air.
Flash states he got the sword from the crystal block.
Ming reacts with shock, claiming only his rightful heir could pull it.
The sword clashes with Ming's, creating fiery sparks and causing Mongons to flee.

memories.memory_text: I'm up on the platform inside the glass tower, moving quickly—there's no time to waste. The mirror-bright object is pulsing, and the red sun outside makes everything feel like it's burning.
grounded_facts: Zarkov is on a platform inside a glass-paned tower in a greenhouse
There is a mirror-bright object visible inside the tower
The sun is blood red and clouds are moving rapidly across the sky
Silvery helium-filled balloons are floating above the structure, secured by wires

memories.memory_text: I remember slamming the TV off and shouting at that fool newscaster—this wasn't some random cosmic accident, it was an attack, planned by a mind. I could feel the truth of it burning in my chest.
grounded_facts: Zarkov turned off the TV and yelled at the newsman, rejecting the idea that the cosmic disturbances were accidental.
Zarkov declared the event an intentional attack by an intelligent force.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the revolver on Munson when he hesitated—calmly, firmly. I had to make him understand: we were launching now, or Earth was finished.
grounded_facts: Zarkov pulled out a revolver and pointed it at Munson to force compliance.
Zarkov commanded Munson to prepare for launch, stating he couldn't handle the capsule alone.

memories.memory_text: I saw the red bolts striking the greenhouse, glass exploding—I shouted into the chaos, demanding Munson show himself or I’d shoot.
grounded_facts: Zarkov observes the greenhouse being hit by red bolts, causing explosions of glass and foliage.
Zarkov sees Munson and issues a direct verbal threat, demanding he come out or be shot.

memories.memory_text: I had the revolver aimed at Munson's back—he was just walking away, leaving us all to die—I shouted that I couldn't take it off alone, that if he wouldn't come, who else would I find?
grounded_facts: Zarkov is holding a revolver and aiming it at Munson.
Munson is walking away from Zarkov toward a door.
Zarkov shouts that he cannot remove something alone and pleads for cooperation.
Zarkov is on the lower stairs of the tower in the greenhouse.

memories.memory_text: I saw the recognition flash in Dale's eyes when she remembered me from that PEOPLE magazine article — the one that called me insane after I left NASA. I hesitated, then confirmed who I was, watching her try to backtrack her words.
grounded_facts: Dale recognizes Zarkov from a PEOPLE magazine article.
Dale mentions the article described Zarkov as 'utterly' something, then stops herself.
Zarkov responds to her implication of his insanity by prompting her with the words 'Insane? Off my rocker?'
Zarkov confirms his identity with a nod after a brief hesitation.

memories.memory_text: I pointed the gun at Flash and told them to go up the stairs ahead of me. The greenhouse was breaking apart from laser strikes and fire, and I urged them to move quickly. Earth was under attack — we had no time to waste.
grounded_facts: Zarkov points a gun at Flash and orders him and Dale to go up the stairs ahead of him.
Zarkov states that Earth is being attacked and they have no time.
Red laser bolts strike the greenhouse, causing glass to shatter and foliage to catch fire.
Zarkov prods Dale with the revolver to make her move.

memories.memory_text: I tore the foliage aside and revealed the capsule—bright as a mirror, untouched by the storm—then yanked the door open and told them I’d shoot if they didn’t get in.
grounded_facts: Zarkov reveals a seamless, mirror-bright escape capsule by ripping away foliage.
Zarkov opens the capsule door and threatens to shoot Dale and Flash if they do not enter.

memories.memory_text: Dale was backing away, refusing, and Flash was about to jump—so I shoved her hard, off-balance, into the capsule before he could act.
grounded_facts: Dale refuses to enter the capsule and shrinks back.
Flash is poised to jump, possibly to escape or resist.
Zarkov pushes Dale into the capsule to force compliance.

memories.memory_text: I forced Flash to the seat and shouted at him to put his foot on the red pedal—then he attacked me, but I twisted away and hit the switch just as I was falling.
grounded_facts: Zarkov pointed a gun at Flash and ordered him to sit with his foot on the red pedal.
Flash attacked Zarkov, punching him and being slammed back.
While being knocked down, Zarkov dove sideways and activated a switch that started the capsule's whirling.

memories.memory_text: The G-force crushed me against the wall, my face straining, barely able to speak—each word took everything: friendship, the world ending, and the three of us having to stop it.
grounded_facts: After the capsule began whirling, increasing centrifugal force pinned Zarkov to the wall.
Zarkov spoke with extreme effort, his lips moving as he said 'Friendship...Built this to send to them in...Friendship...The end now...End of the world....Unless we...Three...Can...'.
Zarkov's face was white and distorted by G-force before he lost consciousness.

memories.memory_text: I watched my capsule climb into the sky, spinning fast and untouched by their laser fire—each bolt glancing off like sparks against steel.
grounded_facts: The capsule is whirling as it ascends into space.
The capsule is being hit by laser bolts that do not harm it.
The capsule is accelerating upward, successfully leaving Earth's surface.

memories.memory_text: I saw Mongo's molten surface rushing toward us, a sea of amber fire, and I crawled to the controls—my hands shaking as I flipped switches, but nothing responded.
grounded_facts: Zarkov regains consciousness and sees Mongo through the capsule wall
Mongo's surface is depicted as molten lava with erupting flames
Zarkov attempts to reach and operate the control panel
The controls do not respond to his input
The capsule continues its descent despite his efforts

memories.memory_text: By God, we did it! We made it!
grounded_facts: Zarkov exclaims in triumph immediately after the capsule lands, expressing certainty that they have successfully completed their journey.
The statement 'By God, we did it! We made it!' is spoken by Zarkov in the script and reflects his emotional reaction to the landing.

memories.memory_text: I don't know where we are.
grounded_facts: When Flash asks where they have landed, Zarkov responds with 'I don't know,' indicating uncertainty about their location despite the successful arrival.
This response follows directly after his initial exclamation of success, marking a shift from confidence to doubt.

memories.memory_text: I told Flash to extend his hand—it’s the universal gesture of friendship—and seconds later, they slammed a manacle on his wrist and threw him down; they were capturing us, not greeting us.
grounded_facts: Zarkov instructed Flash to extend his hand as a gesture of friendship.
Immediately after, the Red Men of Mongo manacled Flash, Dale, and Zarkov.
The Red Men declared them prisoners and announced they would be taken to Ming the Merciless.

memories.scene_title: 33、INT. PALACE OF HALLS - SERIES OF DISSOLVES
memories.memory_text: I shouted 'You inhuman fiend!' after seeing that armored man throw himself onto his sword—Ming ordered it like it was nothing, just to prove a point.
grounded_facts: Zarkov witnesses an armored man commit suicide by falling on his sword after Ming commands it.
Zarkov reacts by exclaiming 'You inhuman fiend!' in response to Ming's cruelty.

memories.scene_title: 33、INT. PALACE OF HALLS - SERIES OF DISSOLVES
memories.memory_text: I remember thinking how perfectly this leads us to Ming—getting to him is our only chance to save Earth, even if Flash just wants to escape.
grounded_facts: Zarkov states that being taken to Ming is 'perfect' because confronting him is the only hope of saving Earth.
Zarkov contrasts his strategic objective with Flash's immediate desire to get out of the situation.

memories.memory_text: Flash is looking for me. He said he has to find Dale and 'that nut Zarkov' and try to save Earth.
grounded_facts: Flash states he must find Dale and 'that nut Zarkov'.
Flash links finding Zarkov and Dale to the goal of trying to save Earth.

memories.scene_title: 41、INT. GROTTO - HIS POV IN CAVERN - ON A CATWALK
memories.memory_text: I was shackled and dragged by soldiers along the catwalk, forced to witness the seething lake of plasma that flared blindingly with each shovelful of gravel dumped into it.
grounded_facts: Zarkov was heavily shackled and being led by soldiers.
Zarkov stopped and gazed at the seething lake of plasma.
The lake flared blindingly whenever gravel was added.
Zarkov did not see Flash, who was observing him from a distance.

memories.memory_text: I called him a devil—Ming—standing there in that hellish light, forcing plasma toward annihilation, and I begged him to use his science for peace instead, to be remembered as blessed, not cursed.
grounded_facts: Zarkov directly addresses Ming in the plasma cavern
Zarkov uses the word 'Devil' to condemn Ming
Zarkov questions why Ming won't turn his science to peaceful use
Zarkov references the potential for Ming's name to be blessed rather than cursed
The scene takes place in a high, elevated area above the cavern with infernal lighting

memories.memory_text: I saw Earth burning inside that cube—fire sweeping over everything, even the clouds, and then it was gone, reborn with strange new continents. It wasn't just destruction; it was a twisted rebirth, and he called it pleasant.
grounded_facts: Zarkov witnesses a holographic Earth in a transparent cube.
The image shows Earth being consumed by fire, then later reappearing with altered continents.
Ming claims this represents Earth's future destruction and rebirth through his plasma force.

memories.memory_text: I lunged at the crystal block and grabbed the hilt, pulling with all my strength—but the sword wouldn't move. No matter how hard I strained, it was as if it were fused to the stone.
grounded_facts: Zarkov sees a block of crystal with two sword hilts protruding.
He rushes to it while chained and attempts to pull one out.
The sword does not budge despite his desperate effort.

memories.memory_text: I woke to a hand on my shoulder and saw a serving girl leaning over me—then realized it was Dale in disguise, her eyes fierce with urgency beneath that cloak.
grounded_facts: Zarkov was asleep in chains at the start of the scene.
A hand touched Zarkov's shoulder, causing him to wake suddenly.
Zarkov saw a serving girl holding a tray, who then revealed herself to be Dale.
Dale was disguised as a serving girl and initiated contact to free Zarkov.

memories.memory_text: My hands vanished—completely invisible—but I could still feel them. I picked up a pebble and tossed it between my unseen hands to show Dale it wasn't gone, just hidden.
grounded_facts: Zarkov observes his hands are invisible
He demonstrates tactile and motor control by picking up and tossing a pebble with invisible hands
Dale gasps and touches one of the invisible hands

memories.memory_text: I watched my hands slowly reappear, fading back into view as if painted over by invisible ink. I wondered—could we control this effect? But then the hawkmen came.
grounded_facts: Zarkov observes his hands gradually becoming visible again
He expresses curiosity about whether the invisibility effect is controllable
A shadow falls over them, followed by the arrival of hawkmen

memories.memory_text: I saw Flash clinging to the edge, straining to pull Barin up, and when Vultan demanded he let him fall, I spoke — 'Call it what you want, Vultan — compassion — mercy: let us teach you the meaning of these words.'
grounded_facts: Zarkov observes the fight between Flash and Barin on the tilting disc.
Flash defeats Barin and saves him from falling into the abyss.
Vultan orders Flash to drop Barin to his death.
Zarkov responds to Vultan's call for execution with a defense of mercy and compassion.

memories.memory_text: I looked Vultan in the eye and asked him, 'What'll you have -- death or dishonor?' and when he hesitated, I knew the choice was his to make.
grounded_facts: Zarkov is chained and approaches Vultan after Flash and Barin are saved.
Zarkov speaks directly to Vultan with a rhetorical question that challenges his values.
Vultan pauses, then declares his decision to save the Earthlings and defy Ming.

memories.memory_text: I told Flash to sit and wait for my signal—when I say zero, he punches the button. No room for error.
grounded_facts: Zarkov instructed Flash to sit in a specific location.
Zarkov assigned Flash the task of pressing a button upon hearing the word 'zero'.
Zarkov asked if Flash had any questions after giving the instruction.

memories.memory_text: I shouted 'Eureka! I did it!' the moment I saw the blinding blue light fade and felt the helmet turn on its own — Flash was gone, completely invisible, just as the calculations predicted.
grounded_facts: Zarkov initiated a countdown ending in 'ZERO!'
There was a blinding blue light inside the capsule
An invisible force turned the helmet on Zarkov's head
Zarkov exclaimed 'Eureka! I did it!' and told Flash he could come out

memories.memory_text: Right after Flash stepped out and his shadow hit the ground, a terrible whining hum tore through the sky — the earth shook, brown foliage rained down, and Barin screamed at me, 'Zarkov! What have you done?'
grounded_facts: Flash's shadow was visible on the ground after he exited the capsule
A rising whining/humming sound filled the air
The earth trembled
Lion Men, Tree Men, and Tiger Man reacted in terror
Brown smoking foliage fell from above
Barin confronted Zarkov with the line 'Zarkov! What have you done?'

memories.memory_text: I heard the thud of a body falling, then silence—until the scrape of a key in the lock broke it, and the door swung open on its own. Then Flash's voice: 'It's me!'—and in that moment, I knew we weren't doomed after all.
grounded_facts: Zarkov was imprisoned in the dungeon with Barin and the Tiger Man.
Zarkov heard the sound of a body falling and then a key turning in the lock.
The dungeon door opened without visible cause.
Flash's voice announced his presence with the words 'It's me!'

memories.memory_text: I watched the needle edge closer to red, and I knew—no calculations left, no way out. I said the words aloud: we've lost, unless a miracle happens.
grounded_facts: Zarkov observes the plasma contracting into a critical mass.
Zarkov states that Earth is finished when the needle hits the red.
Hope drains from Zarkov's expression.
Zarkov verbally acknowledges defeat, saying 'we've lost' without a miracle.
